tasks.title: Sync root `.env` with `.env.example` (MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES and other config drift)
evidence: internal/config/config.go loads MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES into Config.MaxResponseBytes with a default (10MB).
Root .env.example includes MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES, but root .env does not, creating config drift for local runs.
plan: Decide canonical config list and ensure root .env and .env.example contain the same keys (with safe defaults).
Update docs/usage.md configuration section to match the actual config keys consumed by internal/config/config.go.
Add a small test or lint check (optional) that ensures .env.example and .env stay aligned (keys match).

tasks.title: Add `make ci-slow` (stress test) and align docs with actual CI gate
evidence: Makefile defines `ci: install generate format type-check lint build test-ci` and `test-ci` excludes `internal/e2e` and only runs `cd web && pnpm run test`.
docs/usage.md section "CI Coverage" claims `make ci` covers MCP, scheduler, web preview smoke test, and external auth targets, which is not reflected in Makefile targets.
scripts/stress_test.sh exists and performs real-target checks across CLI + API + MCP + scheduler + web build, but it is not wired into Make targets.
plan: Add a `ci-slow` (or `e2e`) target that runs `scripts/stress_test.sh` (and/or `make test` including internal/e2e) with clear expectations and timeouts.
Update docs/usage.md and README.md to clearly separate `make ci` (fast local gate) from `make ci-slow` (real targets/headless).
Ensure `make ci` remains deterministic and reasonably fast; keep network/headless in the slow target.
Add minimal validation to `scripts/stress_test.sh` so failures are actionable (preflight checks, clearer output).
Run make ci and (optionally) make ci-slow.

tasks.title: Harden `scripts/stress_test.sh` prerequisites and remove implicit `python` dependency
evidence: scripts/stress_test.sh shells out to `python -c` for JSON parsing (job status/id extraction), which may not exist as `python` on many systems (python3 only).
scripts/stress_test.sh also assumes curl, sed, and make/pnpm are available but does not preflight or explain missing deps beyond generic failures.
plan: Add a preflight section that checks required commands and prints actionable install hints (go, pnpm, curl, etc.).
Replace `python -c` usage with a more predictable approach (prefer `jq` if present, or a small Node one-liner using the repo’s Node toolchain).
Ensure `--help` documents prerequisites explicitly and includes at least one end-to-end example.
If `make ci-slow` is added, wire it to use the improved script and keep outputs stable.
Run `scripts/stress_test.sh --help` and (optionally) a short smoke run against example.com.

tasks.title: Docs parity sweep: CLI commands and API endpoints vs actual router
evidence: docs/usage.md "Global entry points" list omits commands that exist in internal/cli/cli.go routing (templates, crawl-states, jobs, health).
docs/usage.md API endpoint list does not include every route registered by internal/api/server.go (e.g., /v1/schedules, /v1/templates, /v1/crawl-states).
plan: List all CLI commands from internal/cli/help.go and internal/cli/cli.go and ensure docs/usage.md reflects them with concise examples.
List all API routes from internal/api/server.go and ensure docs/usage.md endpoint list matches (or justify omissions).
Verify api/openapi.yaml covers all API routes (and update if gaps exist).
Run make generate to ensure TS client remains in sync after any OpenAPI edits.

tasks.title: Fix README web-dev proxy docs (use web/.env VITE_API_BASE_URL, not root PORT)
evidence: README.md states the Web UI dev server proxies to `PORT` from root `.env`.
web/vite.config.ts uses VITE_API_BASE_URL (or defaults to http://localhost:8741) and does not read the root `.env`.
plan: Update README.md Web UI section to point users at `web/.env` / `web/.env.example` and VITE_API_BASE_URL for proxy target.
Clarify how to run backend on a non-default port and how to configure the web proxy accordingly.
Run make ci to ensure docs-only change doesn’t break formatting/linting.

tasks.title: Add `spartan version` and unify version across CLI, OpenAPI, and MCP
evidence: internal/mcp/server.go responds to initialize with a hard-coded version string "0.1.0".
CLI help (internal/cli/help.go) does not expose a version command or build metadata.
plan: Introduce a single version source (e.g., internal/buildinfo with Version/Commit/Date variables set via ldflags).
Add a `spartan version` subcommand and include version in `spartan --help` output (or top banner).
Plumb version into MCP initialize response and OpenAPI info.version (generate-time or runtime).
Update Makefile build to pass ldflags for version/commit/date.
Add tests that assert version is surfaced in CLI output and MCP initialize response.
Run make ci.

tasks.title: Remove stray empty directory `spartan-scraper/internal/api/` from repo
evidence: Repository contains an empty `spartan-scraper/internal/api/` directory at the root (separate from `internal/api/`), which looks like an accidental artifact.
plan: Confirm whether the directory is tracked in git (and why it exists).
Remove the directory if it is accidental; ensure no tooling depends on it.
If the directory is generated, add/adjust ignore rules so it never shows up in clean checkouts.
Run make ci.
